commentary_text: The model favors Japan to win at 69% while the sportsbook consensus stands at 46%, a 23-percentage-point gap on the home side. The market simultaneously raises Sweden's win probability to 27% from the model's 7%, suggesting bookmakers see substantially more viability in an upset. This hedging could reflect recent lineup news, injury concerns affecting either squad, or form trends the statistical model cannot capture since its last update. The dashboard surfaces this divergence as a signal: both sources agree Japan enters as favorite, but they disagree sharply on how dominant that advantage is, warranting closer attention to team-news developments before kickoff.
